---
title: "Terminal"
subtitle: "A retro-terminal theme for Quarto reveal.js"
format: terminal-revealjs
author: Eric Nantz
date: last-modified
terminal-path: "quarto-revealjs-terminal"
---

## WELCOME TO THE TERMINAL. {.section-break terminal-path="intro" terminal-status="00 / 09"}

[// QUARTO REVEALJS THEME]{.kicker}

[Slides that look like your favorite shell.]{.lede}

[This deck is built with the theme itself — every slide you see is a working
example you can copy. Press → to begin.]{.desc}

## WHAT YOU GET. {terminal-path="intro/overview" terminal-status="1.1"}

[A complete look-and-feel, ready out of the box.]{.lede}

::: callouts

- **Dark, monospace, pixel-glow headings** — the classic terminal aesthetic
- A **top status bar** and a **bottom footer** with a live slide counter
- **Progress dots**, a CRT **scanline** overlay, and an amber heading **glow**
- Purpose-built **layouts** for callouts, steps, and cards
- **13 color schemes** — and full SCSS control over everything else

:::

## THE BUILDING BLOCKS. {terminal-path="layouts/spans" terminal-status="2.1"}

Everything is plain Markdown plus a few helper spans:

::: callouts

- `[// LABEL]{.kicker}` — an uppercase kicker label
- `[subtitle]{.lede}` — a larger subtitle line
- `[supporting text]{.desc}` — a muted description line
- `[broke]{.neg}` is red, `[works]{.ok}` is green

:::

So a build that was once [broken]{.neg} now [works]{.ok}.

## STEP BY STEP. {terminal-path="layouts/steps" terminal-status="2.2"}

[Wrap an ordered list in `::: steps` for numbered boxes.]{.lede}

::: steps

1. Install the theme with `quarto add rpodcast/quarto-revealjs-terminal`
2. Set `format: terminal-revealjs` in your front matter
3. Write Markdown — add helper spans where you want emphasis
4. Render with `quarto render` and ship it

:::

## SIDE BY SIDE. {terminal-path="layouts/cards" terminal-status="2.3"}

::: cards

::: card
[CALLOUTS]{.card-title}
Turn any bullet list into bordered callout boxes by wrapping it in a
`::: callouts` div.

Great for **key points** and takeaways.
:::

::: card
[CARDS]{.card-title}
Place several `::: card` divs inside `::: cards` for a clean two-column layout.

Use `[Title]{.card-title}` — **not** a heading — for the card title.
:::

:::

> Tip: never put a Markdown heading (`##`, `###`) inside a card — Quarto turns
> it into a nested slide and breaks navigation.

## STATUS BAR & FOOTER. {terminal-path="chrome/status" terminal-status="ACTIVE" terminal-hint="set it per slide ↑"}

[The chrome is driven by header attributes.]{.lede}

Set these on any `##` slide header:

::: callouts

- `terminal-path` — the green `>` prompt path, top-left
- `terminal-status` — the status label with a dot, top-right
- `terminal-hint` — overrides the footer hint (see the bottom-right of *this*
  slide)

:::

Document-level defaults live in the YAML front matter, so you only set what
changes per slide.

## CODE & FILE TREES. {terminal-path="content/code" terminal-status="3.1"}

[Fenced code blocks render in a bordered terminal panel.]{.lede}

```text
~/.config/terminal-theme/
├── _quarto.yml         # project settings
├── custom.scss         # your overrides (palette, colors, fonts)
├── slides.qmd          # this presentation
└── _extensions/
    └── terminal/       # the theme lives here
```

Inline `code` is highlighted too — set the accent with `$terminal-accent`.

## PICK A COLOR SCHEME. {terminal-path="theme/palettes" terminal-status="3.2"}

[Switch the whole deck like a modern terminal — one line.]{.lede}

```scss
/*-- scss:defaults --*/
$terminal-palette: "solarized-dark";
```

::: cards

::: card
[DARK SCHEMES]{.card-title}
`amber` · `solarized-dark` · `dracula` · `nord` · `gruvbox-dark` · `monokai` ·
`one-dark` · `catppuccin-mocha` · `tomorrow-night-eighties` · `green-phosphor` ·
`cyan-navy`
:::

::: card
[LIGHT SCHEMES]{.card-title}
`solarized-light` · `gruvbox-light`

Light palettes automatically soften the glow and scanlines so text stays
readable.
:::

:::

## MAKE IT YOURS. {.section-break terminal-path="theme/customize" terminal-status="SECTION 4 / 4"}

[// FULL SCSS CONTROL]{.kicker}

[Start from a palette, then override anything.]{.lede}

[`$terminal-accent`, `$terminal-bg`, `$terminal-heading-font`, `$terminal-glow`,
`$terminal-scanlines` — every color, font, and effect is a variable.]{.desc}

## START BUILDING. {terminal-path="quarto-revealjs-terminal" terminal-status="DONE" terminal-hint="that's all — go build"}

[Three commands and you're presenting.]{.lede}

::: steps

1. `quarto use template rpodcast/quarto-revealjs-terminal`
2. Edit the generated `.qmd` — this file is your reference
3. `quarto render` → open the HTML → present

:::

Questions, issues, and contributions are **welcome** on
[GitHub](https://github.com/rpodcast/quarto-revealjs-terminal).
